mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls indicates contracting labor income and consumption expectations, causing equity risk premiums to expand and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward test shows that months with negative payroll changes do not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns (t-statistic > -1.5).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, the economy is in a late-cycle contraction where rising joblessness reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 4.5%, positive 60-day SPX returns occur in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 4% while industrial production is simultaneously declining month-over-month, the interaction of tight credit and weakening output amplifies recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of FEDFUNDS > 4% and INDPRO decline do not produce significantly more negative 20-day SPX returns than either condition alone (F-test for interaction p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1400 thousand units signal a collapsing residential investment sector; in a demand-led downturn this reduces construction multipliers and durable goods orders, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Periods with HOUST below 1400 during demand-led downturns fail to produce negative average 60-day SPX returns (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production confirm a contraction in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, leading to negative earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Instances of two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% maintains restrictive borrowing costs for financial intermediaries, triggering deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weeks following observations of FEDFUNDS > 4% do not show negative average 5-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals resilient consumer demand and supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Months with positive RSAFS changes do not produce positive average 20-day SPX returns (t-statistic < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 315 indicate elevated inflation that compresses real household incomes and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain tight policy, reducing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarters with CPIAUCSL above 315 do not produce negative average 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC > 0.03).
